twitter_thread: 6 months, 50 dividend stocks: only 10 pay 4%+ yield.
Volatility kills dividend returns. Use a 200-day MA to avoid 20%+ drawdowns.
3M and Coca-Cola have hiked payouts 64 and 59 years straight, respectively.
Kinder Morgan's 75% dividend cut in 2020 wiped out 5 years of returns.
Realty Income and National Retail Properties yield 4.2% and 4.5% with 10% volatility.
Screen for payout ratios <60% and interest coverage >5x to avoid traps.
What's your worst dividend investing mistake? #dividendstocks #passiveincome
pack.linkedin: After 6 months analyzing 50 dividend stocks, I found only 10 pay 4%+ yield. To maximize returns, use a 200-day moving average to avoid 20%+ drawdowns and focus on stalwarts like 3M and Coca-Cola, which have increased payouts 64 and 59 years straight. Avoid disasters like Kinder Morgan's 75% dividend cut in 2020. What's your approach to dividend investing?
tiktok_hooks: 10 out of 50 dividend stocks pay 4%+ yield, the rest are mediocre
20% drawdowns can kill your dividend returns, use a 200-day MA
4.2% yield with 10% volatility, that's the dividend sweet spot
